title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs incremental state machines, concurrency, cancellation, and ownership from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.
Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.
Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Incremental State Machine · changed case · Review Incremental State Machine
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 6992 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.
